MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556

  • Release Notes
ReadWriteEDID C++

ReadWriteEDID illuminates how to export the EDID of specified channel as a bin file, and how to import a specified EDID to a specified channel.
Folder dir: SDKv3\Examples\Legacy_VC++\CmdTools\ReadWriteEDID

Supported Hardware

Pro, Eco and USB Gen2/Plus capture devices that support HDMI signals

Purpose

  1. Save EDID of the specified channel as a .bin file.
  2. Read the .bin file from the specified location to set the EDID of specified channel.

Workflow

  1. To get device version, initialize and enumerate all available devices, call: MWGetVersion, MWCaptureInitInstance, MWGetChannelCount
  2. To find the valid capture card according to the device info,, call: MWGetChannelInfoByIndex

  3. Conduct any of the following steps to open a channel:

    1. To open the specified channel according to the command parameter, call:MWOpenChannel;
    2. To open the first available channel by default when there is not a specified channel, call:MWGetDevicePath, MWOpenChannelByPath;

  4. To import or export the EDID file of current channel according to the command value. If the command value contains a file path, the EDID file of the path will be imported to your capture device; if not, the EDID data of your device will be exported to the .bin file at the default folder.:

    1. To export the EDID file of your specified channel and save it as a BIN file.
      1. Obtain the EDID, call: MWGetEDID;
      2. Save as a BIN file.
    2. To import a BIN file to your specified channel.
      1. Read the BIN file, call: fread;
      2. Write the EDID file to the specified channel, call: MWSetEDID;
  5. To free up system resources and memory, call: MWCloseChannel,MWCaptureExitInstance

Result

Figure 1 Export EDID of the specified channel



Figure 2 Import EDID into the specified channel
